title: Warm Slovakian Bread Flour Casserole
description: A fluffy, one-pot Slovakian casserole made with bread flour, vegetables, and a touch of wok-hei for a satvik-friendly twist.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine bread flour, salt, sugar, and active dry yeast; mix until evenly distributed.
Add warm water and olive oil to the dry mixture; stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a lightly floured surface for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise for 30 minutes until doubled in size.
While dough rises, preheat oven to 180°C.
After dough has risen, punch it down, roll it out into a rectangular shape, and transfer it to a greased casserole dish.
Arrange diced carrots, sweet potato, and sprinkle wok-hei evenly over the dough; drizzle with a little olive oil and season with salt.
Bake uncovered for 45 minutes until the top is golden brown and a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean; let it cool for 5 minutes before serving warm.
